ednc.name: Eastern District of North Carolina
ednc.rulesTitle: Local Civil Rules of Practice and Procedure

wdnc.name: Western District of North Carolina
wdnc.rulesTitle: Rules of Practice and Procedure (2017 ed., as revised)

meta.disclaimer: Local rules are amended periodically. Always verify against the current PDF before filing. Judges' standing orders and case-specific scheduling orders may supersede defaults.

ednc.value: 21 days after service of the motion

mdnc.value: 21 days after service of the motion

wdnc.value: 14 days after service of the motion

rules.topic: Response to a summary-judgment / dispositive motion

ednc.value: 21 days (same as other nondiscovery motions)

mdnc.value: 30 days after service of the motion

wdnc.value: 14 days (no separate SJ deadline; judge's standing order may vary)

ednc.value: 14 days after service

mdnc.value: 14 days after service

wdnc.value: 14 days (no separate discovery-motion deadline)

ednc.value: 14 days after service of response (replies "discouraged")

mdnc.value: 14 days after service of response (limited to new matter)

wdnc.value: 7 days after service of response; surreplies not allowed without leave

ednc.value: Replies not permitted in discovery disputes

mdnc.value: 7 days after service of response

wdnc.value: 7 days after service of response

rules.topic: Deadline to file motions (after close of discovery)

ednc.value: 30 days after close of discovery (except motions re: trial evidence)

mdnc.value: Notice of intent to file dispositive motion within 14 days; dispositive motion within 30 days after close of discovery

wdnc.value: Set by the presiding judge's Scheduling / Case Management Order

ednc.value: Mandatory planning meeting; Rule 26(f) report required (court form in Rule 26.1(e)(2))

mdnc.value: Rule 26(f) meeting at least 14 days before initial pretrial conference; joint or separate report filed within 10 days of meeting

wdnc.value: Initial Attorneys' Conference (IAC) within 14 days of joinder of issues; Certificate of IAC filed within 7 days of IAC

ednc.value: Set by scheduling order after 26(f) report (no default track in local rules)

mdnc.value: Three tracks: Standard 4 months; Complex 6 months; Exceptional 9 months (from commencement date in IPTO)

wdnc.value: Set by the Scheduling Order; court-enforceable discovery does not commence until Scheduling Order issues

ednc.value: Good cause + good-faith conferral with affected parties/nonparties; proposed order required

mdnc.value: Governed by LR 6.1; no brief required if timely

wdnc.value: No brief required for timely extension motions; consultation not required for initial-response extensions

rules.topic: Objections to magistrate-judge R&R — length limit

ednc.value: No district-specific length cap; use Rule 7.2(f) memoranda limits

mdnc.value: 6,250 words / 20 pages

wdnc.value: No local-rule length cap; follows default LCvR 7.1(d) 25-page limit

ednc.value: 30 pages OR 8,400 words

mdnc.value: 6,250 words (computer) or 20 pages (typewriter/handwritten)

wdnc.value: 25 pages (default; judge's standing order may alter)

ednc.value: 10 pages OR 2,800 words

mdnc.value: 3,125 words (computer) or 10 pages (typewriter/handwritten)

wdnc.value: No separate cap; within overall 25-page default (surreplies disallowed absent leave)

ednc.value: 10 pages OR 2,800 words

mdnc.value: Same as other briefs (6,250 / 20 pages); expedited resolution may be agreed under LR 37.1(b) with no briefing

wdnc.value: 25 pages default (no separate discovery-motion cap)

ednc.value: 30 pages / 8,400 words (principal); 10 pages / 2,800 words (reply)

mdnc.value: 6,250 words (principal/response) or 20 pages; 3,125 words (reply) or 10 pages

wdnc.value: 25 pages default (judge's standing order may vary)

ednc.value: Yes — required when using the word-limit option; must state word count and certify compliance

mdnc.value: Yes — every brief must include a certificate of word count

wdnc.value: Not required by local rule (local rule uses pages)

ednc.value: No specific length cap; filed 7 days before trial with motions re: evidence admissibility

mdnc.value: 6,250 words / 20 pages; filed at least 21 days before trial

wdnc.value: Set by presiding judge / Case Management Order

ednc.value: At least 11-point font, double-spaced, 1-inch margins on all sides, 8½ × 11 paper, single-sided

mdnc.value: Proportional font ≥ 13 point OR fixed-pitch ≥ 10 chars/inch; double-spaced; top margin ≥ 1¼", other margins ≥ 1"; max 27 lines per page; single-sided

wdnc.value: Font ≥ 12 point, double-spaced, 1-inch margins, each page numbered (default; Standing Civil Order may vary)

ednc.value: Name, firm, address, phone, fax, email, state bar no. of all filing attorneys; printed name below signature

mdnc.value: Telephone, mailing address, email; state bar number for attorneys admitted to this Court

wdnc.value: Not separately specified in civil rule; follow Administrative Procedures / standing orders

ednc.value: Not permitted; will not be considered. Condensed deposition transcripts discouraged.

mdnc.value: Pages must be single-sided (applies generally)

wdnc.value: Not specified; exhibits must be numbered and filed as separate attachments

rules.topic: Notice of supplemental / subsequently decided authority

ednc.value: Citation only (copy of opinion if unpublished); no argument

mdnc.value: Citation only (copy of opinion if unpublished); no argument

wdnc.value: Notice must state reasons + page of brief; ≤ 2 pages; response similarly limited

ednc.value: Not a general rule for civil cases; follow assigned judge's practice preferences (specific rule exists for bankruptcy appeals, L.Civ.R. 83.7(b))

mdnc.value: Paper copies only as required by presiding judge's Judicial Preferences page or by court order

wdnc.value: Not specified; follow Administrative Procedures and standing orders

ednc.value: Yes — electronic filing required for registered users; specific exceptions (pro se, sealed, certain criminal papers)

mdnc.value: Yes — all documents must be filed electronically except listed exceptions (pro se, sealed, Qui Tam, trial exhibits, certain criminal papers)

wdnc.value: Yes — governed by LCvR 5.2.1 and the Administrative Procedures

ednc.value: Motion to seal required; specific procedure in L.Civ.R. 79.2; medical records must be accompanied by motion to seal

mdnc.value: Sealing disfavored; LR 5.5 Report required (meet-and-confer); LR 5.4 Checklist + Summary Chart to judge's ECF mailbox within 3 business days

wdnc.value: Motion to seal (separate from underlying pleading) with specific findings; public notice via docket

ednc.value: Required with motions for extension of time and consented/unopposed nondispositive motions; generally required with motion unless clerk may act

mdnc.value: Required for motions not requiring briefs (LR 7.3(j)); email to judge's ECF mailbox

wdnc.value: Not required unless court orders; submit via Cyberclerk if directed

ednc.value: Attach (i) proposed amended pleading (signed, with exhibits) and (ii) redline showing changes; incarcerated pro se exempt from redline

mdnc.value: Attach the proposed amended pleading to the motion (no redline required by rule)

wdnc.value: Not separately specified by local rule

ednc.value: Required for extension motions (L.Civ.R. 6.1) and discovery motions (good-faith certification, L.Civ.R. 7.1(c)(2))

mdnc.value: Required for discovery motions; certificate must state date, participants, and specific results achieved

wdnc.value: Required for all civil motions (Certificate of Consultation), with exceptions for dispositive motions, represented-vs-pro-se, and initial-response extensions

ednc.value: Motions decided without hearing unless court orders otherwise; hearings at court's discretion

mdnc.value: Decided on papers unless court orders otherwise; clerk gives ≥ 7 days' notice of oral argument

wdnc.value: Motions ordinarily ruled on without oral argument

rules.topic: One motion per document; no motions in responsive briefs

ednc.value: Motions shall be concise and state relief requested; no explicit "separate document" rule

mdnc.value: Each motion in a separate document; related counsel must join in a single brief subject to length limits

wdnc.value: Motions must not be included in responsive briefs; each motion as a separately filed pleading

ednc.value: Required: separate numbered statement of material facts; opposing party must respond in correspondingly numbered paragraphs; unchallenged facts deemed admitted; appendix of evidence required

mdnc.value: Statement-of-elements approach (not a separate numbered-fact statement); briefs must set out elements and authenticated facts

wdnc.value: Not separately required by local rule; follow judge's standing order

ednc.value: Good-faith certification required; motion must attach specific question/objection with grounds

mdnc.value: Personal-consultation certificate required: date, participants, specific results achieved

wdnc.value: General Certificate of Consultation required on all civil motions (see "meet-and-confer" row)

ednc.value: All discovery motions handled on expedited briefing: 14-day response; no replies; oral argument by phone permitted

mdnc.value: By agreement, 30-minute telephone conference or 1-hour in-court hearing before magistrate judge without briefing

wdnc.value: Judges may set expedited procedures in their Scheduling Order (2015 FRCP 16 amendment)

ednc.value: No district-wide cap (parties propose in Rule 26(f) report; default Fed. R. Civ. P. 33 = 25)

mdnc.value: Presumptive caps by track: Standard 15; Complex 25; Exceptional 30 (includes subparts; same for requests for admission)

wdnc.value: Set in Scheduling Order / Pretrial Order; no local-rule numeric cap

ednc.value: No district-wide cap (parties propose in Rule 26(f) report; default Fed. R. Civ. P. 30 = 10)

mdnc.value: Presumptive caps by track (per side, including experts): Standard 4; Complex 7; Exceptional 10

wdnc.value: Set in Scheduling Order / Pretrial Order; no local-rule numeric cap

ednc.value: No detailed local guidelines (follow Fed. R. Civ. P. 30)

mdnc.value: Specific guidelines: no instructions not to answer except on privilege; succinct objections; no off-record conferences except on privilege; deposing counsel must provide witness's counsel copy of documents shown

wdnc.value: No detailed local guidelines (follow Fed. R. Civ. P. 30)

rules.topic: Filing discovery materials with the court

ednc.value: Do not file discovery materials unless ordered or needed for proceedings

mdnc.value: Do not file unless ordered or needed; party seeking discovery relief must attach the specific relevant portion

wdnc.value: Do not file unless (1) ordered, (2) needed in-court, or (3) filed to support/oppose motion

ednc.value: Treated as discovery motion (14-day response, no reply)

mdnc.value: 14-day response; 7-day reply; specific good-cause showing required

wdnc.value: Follow general motion deadlines (14-day response, 7-day reply)

ednc.value: Mediation mandatory in certain cases; parties may address in Rule 26(f) report

mdnc.value: Mediated settlement conferences conducted during discovery under LR 16.4 and LR 83.9a–g

wdnc.value: Mandatory mediated settlement conference in all civil actions unless otherwise ordered; governed by N.C. Supreme Court Mediation Rules as modified

ednc.value: Governed by Local Criminal Rules; not included in civil rules

mdnc.value: Governed by Local Criminal Rules (Feb. 5, 2024)

wdnc.value: 7 days after service of the motion, unless otherwise directed
